sentence: The _____ of the old system caused many problems.
explanation: REGULAR → irregularity (ir- + regular + -ity = lack of regularity)

sentence: His _____ to accept criticism was his biggest weakness.
explanation: WILLING → unwillingness (un- + willing + -ness = not being willing)

sentence: The _____ of the handwriting made the document hard to read.
explanation: LEGIBLE → illegibility (il- + legibil + -ity = quality of being unreadable)

sentence: Her _____ behaviour at the meeting shocked everyone.
explanation: PROFESSION → unprofessional (un- + profession + -al = not professional)

sentence: The _____ of the evidence made the case difficult to prove.
explanation: SUFFICIENT → insufficiency (in- + sufficienc + -y = lack of sufficiency)

sentence: The politician's _____ was exposed by the media.
explanation: SINCERE → insincerity (in- + sincer + -ity = lack of sincerity)

sentence: The _____ of the new law was debated in parliament.
explanation: PRACTICE → impracticality (im- + practical + -ity = quality of being impractical)

sentence: His _____ to follow the rules resulted in his dismissal.
explanation: RESPONSIBLE → irresponsibility (ir- + responsibil + -ity = lack of responsibility)

sentence: The _____ of the old bridge was a major safety concern.
explanation: STABLE → instability (in- + stabil + -ity = lack of stability)

sentence: She showed remarkable _____ in the face of adversity.
explanation: SELF → selflessness (self + -less + -ness = quality of being selfless)

sentence: The _____ of the data led to incorrect conclusions.
explanation: RELIABLE → unreliability (un- + reliabil + -ity = lack of reliability)

sentence: His _____ to the new environment was surprisingly quick.
explanation: ADAPT → adaptability (adapt + -abil + -ity = quality of being adaptable)

sentence: The _____ of the situation was not immediately apparent.
explanation: REVERSE → irreversibility (ir- + reversibil + -ity = quality of being irreversible)

sentence: Her _____ towards her colleagues was unacceptable.
explanation: CONSIDER → inconsiderateness (in- + considerate + -ness = quality of being inconsiderate)

sentence: The _____ of the two accounts raised suspicions.
explanation: CONSISTENT → inconsistency (in- + consistenc + -y = lack of consistency)

sentence: The _____ of the old machinery was a constant problem.
explanation: PREDICT → unpredictability (un- + predictabil + -ity = quality of being unpredictable)

sentence: His _____ to complete the task on time was disappointing.
explanation: ABLE → inability (in- + abil + -ity = lack of ability)

sentence: The _____ of the new policy was questioned by experts.
explanation: ENFORCE → unenforceability (un- + enforceabil + -ity = quality of being unenforceable)

sentence: She demonstrated great _____ in managing the crisis.
explanation: RESOURCE → resourcefulness (resource + -ful + -ness = quality of being resourceful)

sentence: The _____ of the old system was finally acknowledged.
explanation: ADEQUATE → inadequacy (in- + adequac + -y = state of being inadequate)

sentence: His _____ to the team's success was widely recognised.
explanation: DISPENSE → indispensability (in- + dispensabil + -ity = quality of being indispensable)

sentence: The _____ of the evidence was challenged in court.
explanation: ADMISSIBLE → inadmissibility (in- + admissibil + -ity = quality of being inadmissible)

sentence: Her _____ in dealing with clients was commendable.
explanation: TIRE → tirelessness (tire + -less + -ness = quality of being tireless)

sentence: The _____ of the old regulations was long overdue.
explanation: MODERN → modernisation (modern + -isation = the process of making modern)

sentence: The _____ of the results across different studies was encouraging.
explanation: REPRODUCE → reproducibility (reproduce + -ibil + -ity = quality of being reproducible)
